round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent effectively synthesized cross-domain evidence (landscape, botanics, meta) to refine its confidence from medium to high for Uganda.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent integrated regulatory evidence (LHT) and linguistic evidence to strengthen the case for Uganda over other regional candidates.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent used regulatory (LHT) and linguistic (English) evidence to rule out DRC and Rwanda, significantly improving the specificity of its conclusion.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent incorporated landscape and meta evidence (soil, architecture) to distinguish Uganda from Kenya and Rwanda.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent synthesized landscape and regulatory evidence to confirm the specific regional characteristics of the utility poles and architecture.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge provided a clear, structured synthesis of all five agents' evidence. It correctly identified the key visual features (English text, red soil, banana plants, LHT, specific utility poles) and logically combined them to reach the correct conclusion (Uganda). The reasoning explicitly addressed why other candidates were less likely.
